subject: Bump pysnmp requirement version
commit_message:
Bump pysnmp requirement version

Currently used pysnmp version can't be used with Python >= 3.7 version due to new keyword usage.
Add py37, py38 and py39 envs into tox config.

Added mvn-phase-lib locally to test python3 tox

Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-2494
Signed-off-by: Michal Jagiello <michal.jagiello@t-mobile.pl>
Change-Id: I526ceb8d66b40b0a87f0ba688e1ed7f2e3e5b34f
Signed-off-by: vv770d <vv770d@att.com>

Patch Set 1:

@Jessica Wagantall do you know why InterpreterNotFound exception is raised for Python 3.9 tox runner? I've added Python 3.9 support in https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/ci-management/+/117081 but it's still not available

Patch Set 1:

@Michal, can you please request a node update via packer to include your packer change?
(via support.linuxfoundation.org)

Basically, once you merge a change in packer, we need to generate a new node based on those changes and update the node information in https://github.com/onap/ci-management/tree/master/jenkins-config

Patch Set 1:

I can confirm that Python3.9 is installed in this node. Here is the list of packages that ran for this particular node:
https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/dcaegen2-collectors-snmptrap-master-snmptrap-verify-python/4/packages_end.txt.gz

I wonder if the issue is because the virtual env is trying to run using Python2:
2:21:30 ERROR:   py39: InvocationError for command /w/workspace/dcaegen2-collectors-snmptrap-master-snmptrap-verify-python/venv-tox/bin/python2 -m virtualenv --no-download --python /usr/bin/python3.9 py39 (exited with code 1)

Is there a way that you guys can force venv-tox/bin/python3 or something like that?

>

> https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/dcaegen2-collectors-snmptrap-master-snmptrap-verify-python/4/packages_end.txt.gz

> 2:21:30 ERROR:   py39: InvocationError for command /w/workspace/dcaegen2-collectors-snmptrap-master-snmptrap-verify-python/venv-tox/bin/python2 -m virtualenv --no-download --python /usr/bin/python3.9 py39 (exited with code 1)
> 
> Is there a way that you guys can force venv-tox/bin/python3 or something like that?


I also tried to debug it and in my case I could create Python3.9 virtualenv using Python2.7, but we can check. If we want to do that we should change https://github.com/onap/dcaegen2-platform/blob/master/mvn-phase-lib.sh#L125 to use Python3.X explicitly using `python3.X -m venv ./venv-tox`

This is actually getting pulled from nexus raw (source is from https://git.onap.org/dcaegen2/utils/tree/scripts/mvn-phase-lib.sh#n225)

To simplify test here, we can bring that mvn-phase-lib.sh locally into this repo and modify it and update mvn-phase-script.sh to not pull from nexus

We do not have any python2 settings in the maven global-settings. We only have definitions for the Nexus repositories. 

I think Python2 is being selected from when the venv in created:
virtualenv $WORKSPACE/venv-tox

Can we try creating a python3 venv rather?
https://help.dreamhost.com/hc/en-us/articles/115000695551-Installing-and-using-virtualenv-with-Python-3
